channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A fierce storm is lashing Ireland and Scotland with record winds, downing power lines
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ireland was hit with wind gusts of 114 miles (183 kilometers) an hour, the strongest on record, as a winter storm battered the country and northern parts of the U.K. on Friday, leaving hundreds of thousands of people without power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Killed for fighting corruption, a Congolese man made a martyr is inspiring a new generation
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — When Floribert Bwana Chui Bin Kositi was asked in 2007 to allow spoiled rice from Rwanda to be transported across the border to the eastern Congo city of Goma, he knew the risks of resisting corruption, especially as a government worker. He refused nonetheless.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Afghan women’s group hails court's move to arrest Taliban leaders for persecution of women
item.description: An Afghan women’s group on Friday hailed a decision by the International Criminal Court to arrest Taliban leaders for their persecution of women.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump targets California water policy as he prepares to tour LA fire damage
item.description: President Donald Trump is criticizing California water policy as he prepares to tour wildfire damage in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Amy Taxin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After 80 years, not many Auschwitz survivors are left. One man makes telling the stories his mission
item.description: HAIFA, Israel (AP) — Naftali Fürst will never forget his first view of the Auschwitz-Birkenau concentration camp, on Nov. 3, 1944. He was 12 years old. SS soldiers threw open the doors of the cattle car, where he was crammed in with his mother, father, brother, and more than 80 others.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Democrats' Minnesota House boycott echoes earlier walkouts in other states
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Democrats in the Minnesota House who have boycotted daily sessions are using tactics that lawmakers around the country have tried at least two dozen times before to thwart their opponents. It's not even a first for the state.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Carolina drunk driver arrested in December had been deported 3 times: affidavit
item.description: An illegal immigrant arrested for drunk driving in South Carolina has a lengthy criminal history in the United States and had been deported three times in less than five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rhode Island judge specializing in immigration law resigns after FBI raids office
item.description: The FBI and Providence, Rhode Island, police raided a Central Falls Municipal Court judge's office Thursday, though the reasons are unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Convicted Arkansas OnlyFans actor arrested by US Marshals in Florida
item.description: A Van Buren porn actor who was sentenced in December for human trafficking and other charges has been arrested in Florida for violating the terms of his release, according to U.S. Marshals Service.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Florida man arrested after allegedly climbing over wall at Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate
item.description: A man allegedly climbed over the wall that surrounds President Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate in Palm Beach, Florida, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schumer calls on Trump to declassify government files on UFOs
item.description: “Now do UFOs,” Schumer wrote on social platform X in a repost of the White House’s announcement that Trump has ordered government files on John F.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: American hiker shot by suspected Mexican cartel while on US soil
item.description: U.S. Border Patrol El Centro Sector officials confirmed that a hiker was shot and robbed by “suspected cartel members" while exploring an area of San Diego County near the U.S.-Mexico border with a group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LA wildfires: Surveillance video captures suspects stealing fire equipment during disaster response
item.description: Federal investigators are searching for two men who were caught on video stealing critical fire equipment during the Los Angeles wildfire response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles agency reveals estimated economic impact of deadly wildfires as infernos still rage
item.description: Officials on Thursday estimated the L.A. wildfires have caused more than $50 billion in property damage, as fires continue to burn.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gulf of Whatnow? Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump order ending federal DEI programs leaves agencies and stakeholders on uncertain ground
item.description: From federal agencies to stakeholders who get federal dollars for special training, many are trying to process how President Donald Trump’s sweeping executive order putting a stop to diversity, equity and inclusion programs across the U.S. government will upend their work.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, AYANNA ALEXANDER and COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order to declassify files on JFK, RFK and MLK Jr. assassinations
item.description: “Give that to RFK Jr.,” Trump said about the pen he used to sign the order declassifying info about the JFK, RFK and MLK assassinations.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Post Malone teams up with Oreo on limited-edition cookie flavor
item.description: Oreo has partnered with Post Malone for its newest cookie, featuring a first-of-its-kind filling for the brand.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Colorado DNA analyst appears on forgery charges as validity of more than 500 cases in doubt
item.description: GOLDEN, Colo. (AP) — A former Colorado Bureau of Investigation DNA scientist is facing 102 criminal charges, including dozens of counts of forgery, after authorities said she cut corners and didn't follow standard testing protocols in hundreds of criminal cases.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wall of ice the size of Rhode Island heading toward penguin-packed island off Antarctica
item.description: The world's biggest iceberg — a wall of ice the size of Rhode Island — is lumbering toward a remote island off Antarctica that's home to millions of penguins and seals.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experts worry that Trump's Jan. 6 pardons will legitimize political violence, embolden extremists
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After President Donald Trump pardoned around 1,500 Jan. 6 Capitol rioters on Monday, far-right activists cheered the move and said it strengthened their loyalty to him. Some also borrowed from the president’s own rhetoric, calling for retribution.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate confirms John Ratcliffe as Trump's CIA director
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed John Ratcliffe to lead the Central Intelligence Agency in overwhelming bipartisan fashion, making him the second member of President Trump’s national security team to be approved by the upper chamber. Senators confirmed Ratcliffe in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Mailman murder suspect with history of violence indicted in brazen lunch break attack
item.description: Jaia Cruz, 24, indicted in stabbing murder of US mailman Ray Hodge III on his lunch break at a Manhattan deli earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Murkowski announces she will vote against Hegseth
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-Alaska) announced Thursday afternoon she will vote against Pete Hegseth, President Trump’s nominee to serve as secretary of Defense, citing his past infidelity and allegations of sexual assault and excessive drinking.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alabama man charged with 11 homicides -- including two mass shootings -- pleads not guilty
item.description: An Alabama man charged with homicide in two separate mass shootings pleaded not guilty in state court on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ship stuck in ice off Lake Erie shoreline in Buffalo
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (WIVB) — A lake freighter ship is stuck in ice in the Lake Erie shoreline in the Buffalo River. The vessel Manitoulin, which was returning to Michigan after completing a routine delivery of rock salt, became immobilized by thick ice, which the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KRON4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's renaming plans leave mapmakers wondering what to do next
item.description: President Trump's territorial assertions sparked a round of rethinking by mapmakers and teachers, snark on social media and sarcasm by at least one other world leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nashville police investigate high school shooting suspect's 'very concerning' internet and social media posts
item.description: Details are emerging surrounding Antioch High School shooter Solomon Henderson's alleged online writings as investigators in Tennessee are looking for a motive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
